task.title: Start the interactive REPL
task.shortdesc: The interactive REPL lets you load a Markdown document and explore its structure, test XPath queries, and evaluate rules interactively. Use it when writing new rules to confirm your queries return what you expect before adding them to a rule set.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Start the markdown-validator interactive REPL to explore document structure, test XPath expressions, and evaluate rules before adding them to a rule set.

prereq.p: markdown-validator is installed and the virtual environment is active. See Install markdown-validator.

context.p: The REPL is a scratch pad for rule authors — try queries and rules against real documents without modifying the rule set file. Use md-validate validate for production validation.

step.stepresult: The terminal shows a welcome message and the > prompt: Markdown Validator REPL Type 'help' for a list of commands, 'quit' to exit. >

step.cmd: Load a Markdown document to work with.
step.stepresult: The REPL confirms the file is loaded and shows the front-matter keys: Loaded: /abs/path/docs/tutorial-deploy-app.md Metadata keys: ['title', 'ms.topic', 'ms.date', 'author', 'description']

step.cmd: Inspect the document's metadata and HTML structure before testing rules.

info.p: dump metadata prints the YAML front matter as a JSON object. dump html prints the rendered HTML body, which shows the exact structure your XPath queries will run against.

result.p: The REPL is running with a document loaded. To test rules, see Test a rule in the interactive REPL.

postreq.p: To exit the REPL, enter quit or press Ctrl+D.
